title: Julian Bousquet
wikitext:
{{short description|France international rugby league footballer}} {{Use dmy dates|date=April 2023}} {{Infobox rugby league biography |name = Julian Bousquet |fullname = |image = Julian Bousquet Catalans.jpg |imagesize = 250 |caption = |birth_date = {{birth date and age|1991|07|08|df=y}} |birth_place = [[Fabrezan]], [[Aude]],
[[Occitania (administrative region)|Occitania]], France |death_date = |height = {{convert|1.93|m|ftin|abbr=on|order=flip}} |weight = {{convert|117|kg|stlb|abbr=on|order=flip}}{{cite web|url= http://www.catalansdragons.com/equipe/|title=Catalans Dragons Equipe Saison 2015|author=|year=2015|website=Catalans Dragons site officiel|publisher= Catalans Dragons S.A.S.P.|access-date=30 June 2015}} |position = {{rlp|PR}} |club1 = {{nowrap|[[Lézignan Sangliers]]}} |year1start = 2009 |year1end = 11 |appearances1 = 33 |tries1 = 4 |goals1 = 0 |fieldgoals1 = 0 |points1 = 16 |club2 = [[Catalans Dragons]] |year2start = 2012 |year2end = present |appearances2 = 305 |tries2 = 34 |goals2 = 0 |fieldgoals2 = 0 |points2 = 132 |teamA = [[France national rugby league team|France]] |yearAstart = 2011 |yearAend = present |appearancesA = 17 |triesA = 2 |goalsA = 0 |fieldgoalsA = 0 |pointsA = 8 |new = yes |retired = no |source = [https://www.loverugbyleague.com/stats/players/Julian-Bousquet/ loverugbyleague][http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/julian-bousquet/summary.html RLP][http://www.itsrugby.co.uk/player_18487.html itsrugby] |updated = 27 October 2025 }} '''Julian Bousquet''' (born 8 July 1991) is a French professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}}{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/Julian-Bousquet/summary.html|title=Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=rugbyleagueproject.org|date=31 December 2010|accessdate=2011-01-01}} for the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]] and at international level for [[France national rugby league team|France]]. He previously played in the [[Elite One Championship]] for the [[Lézignan Sangliers]]. ==Background== Bousquet was born in [[Fabrezan]], [[Occitania (administrative region)|Occitanie]], [[France]]. ==Club career== Bousquet began his career at [[Lézignan Sangliers]]. He moved to [[Catalans Dragons]] and made his debut in 2012. In early 2013 he was involved in a controversial incident during a match against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] when he knocked [[Théo Fages]] unconscious with an illegal challenge, receiving a red card and subsequently being banned for four matches. On 30 May 2015, Bousquet signed an extension to his Catalans contract.[http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/9869508/julian-bousquet-signs-new-catalans-dragons-contract Julian Bousquet signs new Catalans Dragons contract] He played in the [[2018 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/aug/25/catalans-dragons-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history|work=The Guardian|date=25 August 2018|accessdate=5 November 2019}} On 9 October 2021, Bousquet played for Catalans in their [[2021 Super League Grand Final]] defeat against St. Helens.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58858850|title=Super League Grand Final: Catalans Dragons 10-12 St Helens: Saints win three in a row|publisher=BBC}} On 14 October 2023, Bousquet played in Catalans [[2023 Super League Grand Final]] loss against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/oct/14/catalans-dragons-wigan-super-league-grand-final-rugby-league-match-report/|title=Wigan Warriors crowned Super League champions after downing Dragons|website=www.theguardian.com}} ==International career== Bousquet made his début for [[France national rugby league team|France]] as a [[rugby league positions#Substitutes|Substitute]] in the 46–10 victory over [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] in the [[2011 Autumn International Series]] match at [[Stade Gilbert Brutus]], [[Perpignan]] on Saturday 29 October 2011.{{cite web|url=http://www.catalansdragons.com/index.php?article=bousquet_julian|title=Profile at catalansdragons.com|publisher=catalansdragons.com|date=31 December 2011|accessdate=2012-01-01}} Bousquet played in all three French matches of the [[2014 European Cup]] campaign. Bousquet appeared for France again in the [[2015 European Cup]] tournament. He also played for France in their mid-tournament test-match against England. He was a part what was considered a 'weakened' French side due to injury and it showed with an appalling showing against their opponents.{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/live/match/43791/report|title=England demolish France 84-4 in record win|publisher=Sky Sports|accessdate=24 October 2015}} Bousquet played in France's lone international fixture of 2016, an [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations#France vs England|end of year test match]] against England in Avignon. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category}} *[http://www.catalansdragons.com/en/fiche-24-bousquet-julian/ Catalans Dragons profile] *[http://www.ffr13.fr/wp-content/uploads/2015/08/julian_bousquet-791x1024.jpg France profile] *[https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/14296889 Catalan (sic) Dragons sign Julian Bousquet and Damien Cardace] * (archived by web.archive.org) [https://web.archive.org/web/20171023174357/http://www.rlwc2017.com/players?team=380#Julian-Bousquet 2017 RLWC profile] *[https://www.superleague.co.uk/player/162/julian%3Cbr-%3Ebousquet SL profile] *[https://www.bbc.co.uk/search?q=%22Julian+Bousquet%22&edgeauth=eyJhbGciOiAiSFMyNTYiLCAidHlwIjogIkpXVCJ9.eyJrZXkiOiAiZmFzdGx5LXVyaS10b2tlbi0xIiwiZXhwIjogMTc1Nzg2MDI3MSwibmJmIjogMTc1Nzg1OTkxMSwicmVxdWVzdHVyaSI6ICIlMkZzZWFyY2glM0ZxJTNEJTI1MjJKdWxpYW4lMkJCb3VzcXVldCUyNTIyIn0.evUt8GjkoLVt28rSsleJp2Ix4-vT76vJBIe3pAKyeBY Search for "Julian Bousquet" at bbc.co.uk] {{Catalans Dragons squad}} {{Catalans Dragons -
